prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 2014 Paris Municipal elections took place on 23 and 30 March 2014, at the same time as other French municipal elections. Anne Hidalgo of the Socialist Party was elected mayor of Paris, becoming the first woman to hold that position. She had been the city's deputy mayor. Hidalgo won with around 55% of the vote in the second round. Her principal opponent was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet of the UMP who had finished ahead of Hidalgo in first round of voting on 23 March. Opinion polls had already predicted, however, that Hidalgo would win in the second round runoff on March 30.The outgoing mayor was the Socialists' Bertrand Delanoë, who did not run for a third term. Control of Paris' twenty arrondissements was also decided in the elections. Ten
B. The Paris Municipal elections of 2014 were held on 23 and 30 March 2014, coinciding with other municipal elections in France. Anne Hidalgo, a member of the Socialist Party, was elected as the first woman to hold the position of mayor of Paris. She won with approximately 55% of the vote in the second round. Her principal opponent was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet of the UMP, who had finished ahead of her in the first round of voting on 23 March. Opinion polls had already predicted Hidalgo would win in the second round runoff on March 30.
C. The Parisian Municipal elections for 2014 occurred on two dates, March 23 and March 30, 2014, coinciding with other municipal elections across France. Anne Hidalgo, representing the Socialist Party, achieved victory and was elected as the mayor of Paris, marking a historic moment as she became the inaugural woman to occupy this significant office. Prior to this, she had served as the city's deputy mayor. Hidalgo secured approximately 55% of the votes during the second round of the election. Her primary competitor was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet from the UMP, who had actually garnered more votes than Hidalgo in the initial round of voting on March 23. Nevertheless, pre-election surveys had already indicated that Hidalgo was favored to emerge victorious in the runoff during the second round on March 30. The incumbent mayor, Bertrand Delanoë, also a Socialist, did not seek re-election for a third term. Additionally, the elections determined the leadership for Paris' twenty arrondissements. Ten

Answer:

Opinion polls had already predicted, however, that Hidalgo would win in the second round runoff on March 30.The outgoing mayor was the Socialists' Bertrand Delanoë, who did not run for a third term. Control of Paris' twenty arrondissements was also decided in the elections. Ten

Her principal opponent was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet of the UMP, who had finished ahead of her in the first round of voting on 23 March. Opinion polls had already predicted Hidalgo would win in the second round runoff on March 30.

Her primary competitor was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet from the UMP, who had actually garnered more votes than Hidalgo in the initial round of voting on March 23. Nevertheless, pre-election surveys had already indicated that Hidalgo was favored to emerge victorious in the runoff during the second round on March 30.
